name: Malpica - Corme Round of The Lighthouses
name_original: Malpica - Corme Vuelta del Los Faros
region_intro: Touring cycling around Malpica De Bergantiños offers routes along Galicia's rugged Costa da Morte, characterized by its wild Atlantic coastline. The region features dramatic cliffs, pristine beaches such as Beo and Area Maior, and lush eucalyptus and pine forests. Cyclists encounter continuous climbs and descents, providing challenging experiences with expansive ocean views. Best touring cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños The most popular touring cycling route is Malpica - Corme Round of The Lighthouses, a 37.0 miles (59.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the rugged coastline, offering views of the ocean and fishing villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Caión – Arteixo Park loop from Arnados, a difficult 31.9 miles (51.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic coastal vistas. Local touring cyclists also love the Praia das Salseiras – Carballo loop from Carballo, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and inland landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Touring cycling around Malpica De Bergantiños is defined by its wild Atlantic coastline, dramatic cliffs, and fishing villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Malpica De Bergantiños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Malpica De Bergantiños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: Malpica De Bergantiños offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 45 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from coastal paths to inland loops.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The touring cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 16 moderate routes and 30 difficult routes, often featuring continuous climbs and descents along the rugged coastline.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes in Malpica De Bergantiños are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For those seeking a less strenuous ride, consider exploring local roads or shorter sections of the main trails.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, Malpica De Bergantiños is well-known for its challenging touring cycling routes. Many routes feature significant elevation gains and losses, providing a rewarding experience for experienced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956667495/malpica-corme-round-of-the-lighthouses" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpica - Corme Round of The Lighthouses&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 60 km with over 1000 meters of elevation change.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Caión – Arteixo Park loop from Arnados&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Salseiras – Carballo loop from Carballo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes and viewpoints can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes offer dramatic coastal landscapes, including wild Atlantic coastline, rugged cliffs, and pristine beaches like Beo and Area Maior. You'll encounter lush eucalyptus and pine forests, and enjoy expansive ocean views from various points, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3619962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sisargas Islands&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks I can visit along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in landmarks. You can cycle past iconic lighthouses like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Nariga Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncudo Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/292397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Adrián&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3785255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Branco Dune&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Malpica De Bergantiños suitable for family touring cycling trips?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs and descents, there are options for families. Look for shorter, moderate routes or sections of trails that avoid the most challenging terrain. The region's beautiful beaches and natural features can make for enjoyable stops during a family ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling trails?
A: Many outdoor areas in Malpica De Bergantiños are dog-friendly, especially natural trails and beaches. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, particularly in protected areas or near livestock. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the chosen route's distance and difficulty.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños is generally during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, with less intense heat than summer and fewer heavy rains than winter, making for more comfortable riding conditions.

Q: How can I access the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: While direct public transport links to specific trailheads might be limited, Malpica De Bergantiños is accessible by bus from larger towns like A Coruña. However, bringing a bicycle on public transport can sometimes be challenging. Consider starting your routes from the town center of Malpica, which is generally well-connected.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in the town of Malpica De Bergantiños itself, as well as in smaller villages and near popular beaches or landmarks along the coast. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally for the most convenient spots to begin your cycling adventure.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for touring cycling on public roads and established trails in Malpica De Bergantiños. However, always respect private property, follow local traffic laws, and adhere to any signage in natural parks or protected areas regarding cycling access.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the opportunity to explore Galicia's authentic maritime culture and dramatic 'Coast of Death'.
